title: IMGUI Modular Windowing
use_for: designing retained window and widget shells for an IMGUI-based framework
defining reusable base windows with optional header and injected content hosts
planning sidebar-driven context switching inside modular IMGUI window structures
defining single-window shell composition independently from host registry policy
rules: build modular windows over IMGUI using retained shell state and explicit geometry resolution
treat Window and Widget as separate framework primitives because their structure and adaptation needs differ
treat modular attachment of Header, Sidebar, ContextView, and nested Vertical/Horizontal layout regions as the primary mechanism of extensible shell composition
make Header optional but supported by the base shell contract
make ContextView the main injected content host of the shell body
treat Sidebar as an optional body module rather than as a separate window system
procedure: Classify the unit as Window or Widget.
Define the shell regions and which are optional.
Define how the shell hosts title, actions, and body content.
Define how context content is injected into the shell.
Define whether sidebar-driven context switching belongs in the body composition.

primary_when: the request is specifically about reusable base windows, widgets, headers, sidebars, or dynamic content hosting inside an IMGUI window shell
must_not_own_when: the request is mainly about generic IMGUI event phases or input routing

solves: how to define a reusable base window shell in a custom IMGUI framework
how to separate shell structure from feature-specific content
how to keep context switching modular through sidebar and content-host composition
how to distinguish full windows from lightweight widgets
does_not_solve: window registry and restore policy across multiple windows

core_principles: The base shell should own structure, while feature modules own only injected content.
Extensible shell composition should happen by attaching Header, Sidebar, ContextView, and nested layout regions as explicit modules.
Header is a shell concern for title and top-level actions, not the owner of business content.
ContextView is the core extension point for replacing or switching active content.
Sidebar is a body-level navigation module, not a separate window category.
Widgets should remain lighter than full windows even when they share shell concepts.
constraints: The shell must remain valid with or without Header.
Context injection must not require changing shell structure.
Window shells must support both plain content and sidebar-plus-content body arrangements.
Widgets must not inherit unnecessary window-level complexity.
decision_rules: Use Window when the UI unit owns a scenario, navigation, or multiple content contexts.
Use Widget when the unit is HUD-like, embedded, or structurally minimal.
Let imgui-windowing own registry, focus, restore, and placement across multiple windows.
Extend one shell by attaching Header, Sidebar, ContextView, and nested layout regions instead of forking new shell types.
Keep title and header actions in Header rather than mixing them into context content.
Place sidebar beside the content host inside the body when context switching matters.
Reuse one shell contract across contexts and replace only the hosted content.

preferred_patterns: retained window shell with background, header slot, and body slot
dedicated content host inside the shell body
optional sidebar composed as a sibling of the context host
nested Vertical/Horizontal layout regions as modular shell-extension points
separate Window and Widget contracts with related but different policies
shell-driven geometry and feature-driven content injection
avoid_patterns: duplicating whole window structures for each context
mixing host-registry rules into shell composition logic
placing feature-specific logic directly in the shell
making sidebar mandatory for all windows
treating widgets as just undersized windows with all the same obligations

recipes.name: Define a modular retained window shell over IMGUI
recipes.when: Use when multiple contexts should share one structural window contract.
steps: Define the background and root shell regions.
Add an optional header region for title and top-level actions.
Define the body with a dedicated content host.
When needed, split the body into sidebar and content-host regions.
Use nested Vertical and Horizontal layout regions to extend the shell without changing its base contract.
Keep context-specific content injected through the host contract instead of changing shell identity.

pitfalls: Treating modular windows as scene-specific one-offs.
Coupling context content directly to shell structure.
Making widgets carry full window responsibilities.
Using sidebar as a separate architectural system instead of a body module.
review_checklist: Is the shell clearly built over IMGUI rather than uGUI components?
Are Window and Widget distinct where their needs differ?
Is shell composition kept separate from host registry and restore policy?
Are Header, Sidebar, ContextView, and nested layout regions treated as the main shell-extension mechanism?
Is Header optional and structurally native to the shell?
Is ContextView the stable host for dynamic content?
Can Sidebar be added without redefining the base window contract?

references.title: Local Reference: IMGUI uGUI-Inspired Composition
references.interpretation: Use this first for modular window shells, widget distinction, and header plus sidebar plus content-host composition in an IMGUI-based framework.
references.why: Use as the primary local guidance for the modular windowing extension.

references.title: Local Reference: IMGUI Composition
references.interpretation: Use this to align the new modular shell model with the domain's existing IMGUI host and slot composition guidance.
references.why: Use to keep the modular windowing extension coherent with the rest of the IMGUI domain.
